name: Avocado

book_claims.claim: Plant-origin monounsaturated fat associated with lower cardiovascular and cancer death rate
book_claims.mechanism: Contains monounsaturated fatty acids that do not increase cholesterol and protect the heart; plant-origin MUFA specifically associated with lower mortality vs animal-origin MUFA

supplementary_research.finding: Prospective study of 110,000+ participants (Pacheco et al., JAHA 2022) found consuming 2+ servings of avocado per week associated with 16% lower risk of cardiovascular disease and 21% lower risk of coronary heart disease compared to non-consumers.

RCT (Wang et al., 2015, JAHA) showed a moderate-fat diet with one avocado per day significantly reduced LDL cholesterol, small dense LDL, and oxidized LDL compared to matched low-fat or moderate-fat diets without avocado.
NHANES analysis (Fulgoni et al., 2013) found avocado consumers had significantly higher nutrient intakes, lower body weight, lower BMI, and reduced metabolic syndrome risk compared to non-consumers.

culinary_pairings.source: Guacamole; acidity balances richness and prevents oxidation
Guacamole, salads; lycopene absorption enhanced by avocado fat
Both provide complementary plant-origin MUFA profiles
Classic guacamole pairing; capsaicin complementary benefits
Avocado toast pairing; fat aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins
Complete protein when combined; traditional bowl pairing
Classic herb pairing in guacamole and salads

seasonality: Hass variety available year-round due to global sourcing. Peak flavour varies by region: California spring-summer, Mexico year-round, Peru winter. Best consumed when slightly soft to gentle pressure.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highly bioavailable; same predominant fatty acid as olive oil. Reduces LDL without lowering HDL.
More potassium per serving than a banana. Well-absorbed; supports blood pressure regulation.
Mix of soluble and insoluble fibre. Among the highest fibre content of any fruit.
Fat-soluble carotenoids highly bioavailable due to avocado's own fat content. Protect against macular degeneration.
Among the richest food sources. Master antioxidant; oral bioavailability debated but food-matrix form may be better absorbed than supplements.

synergies.mechanism: Avocado fat increases lycopene absorption from tomatoes by 2-4x
Fat from avocado increases absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids (beta-carotene, lutein) from greens by 3-5x
